site.tagline: F-6 Marriage Visa Korea — Professional Consulting
site.description: End-to-end guidance on F-6-1, F-6-2, and F-6-3 marriage visa procedures and country-specific document requirements for 87 countries.

hero.eyebrow: Direct consultation by a licensed administrative attorney
hero.title: Korean F-6 Marriage Visa, From Start to Finish
hero.subtitle: We handle marriage registration and F-6 marriage visa applications for spouses from 87 countries — Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines, China, Japan, Russia and more.

step1.title: 1. Free Consultation
step1.desc: Review your situation and your spouse's nationality.

step2.title: 2. Document Preparation
step2.desc: Country-specific document checklist with notarized translation handled in-house.

step3.title: 3. Visa Issuance
step3.desc: We handle filing with Korean Immigration through to visa issuance.

items.title: Licensed attorney handles your case
items.desc: Not a broker — a registered Korean administrative attorney directly reviews and represents you.

items.title: 87-country case database
items.desc: Procedures from Southeast Asia, CIS, Europe, Latin America, Middle East and Africa.

items.title: Latest regulations
items.desc: Updates to the Immigration Act and official manuals are reflected immediately.

f61.label: F-6-1
f61.title: F-6-1 Spouse of a Korean National
f61.summary: For foreign spouses who have legally married a Korean national and intend to reside together in Korea.

f62.label: F-6-2
f62.title: F-6-2 Caretaker of a Korean Child
f62.summary: For foreign nationals who must raise a minor child born of a marriage with a Korean national in Korea.

f63.label: F-6-3
f63.title: F-6-3 Marriage Dissolved Without Fault
f63.summary: For those who can no longer maintain the marriage through no fault of their own and require continued residence in Korea.
